SEARCH
Package com.societegenerale.failover.observable.micrometer

Class MicrometerObservablePublisher

java.lang.Object
com.societegenerale.failover.observable.micrometer.MicrometerObservablePublisher
All Implemented Interfaces:
ObservablePublisher
public class MicrometerObservablePublisher extends Object implements ObservablePublisher
ObservablePublisher that emits real Micrometer meters on every store/recover event.

Complements MdcLoggerObservablePublisher by publishing actual Counter and Timer registrations visible to Prometheus / Grafana.

Meters emitted

  • failover.store.total (Counter) — tags: name, stored
  • failover.recover.total (Counter) — tags: name, recovered, recovery_failed
  • failover.recovery.outcome.total (Counter) — tags: name, domain, method, outcome (recovered | not_recovered | error). Per intercepted method; the source for the failover / recovery / non-recovery rates.
  • failover.recovery.partial.total (Counter) — tags: name, method. A scatter/gather recover where some (not all) slices were recovered.
  • failover.exception.total (Counter) — tags: name, exception_type (the aspect's wrapper), cause_type (first-level cause), final_cause_type (innermost / actual cause)
  • failover.operation.duration (Timer) — tags: name, action — only when the Metrics bag carries a failover-duration-ns key
  • failover.store.async.failed (Counter) — tags: name, operation, exception_type — emitted when an async store/delete/cleanByExpiry fails inside the executor thread (the async store layer is otherwise visible only in logs)

Tag cardinality

Boolean tags (stored, recovered, recovery_failed) have cardinality 2. exception_type, cause_type and final_cause_type use class canonical names and are expected to be low-cardinality in practice. Exception messages are intentionally never used as tags.

Event discrimination

Operational events (store/recover) carry a failover-action key. Startup report events do not, and are silently ignored by this publisher (they are handled by FailoverMeterBinder).
Author:
Anand Manissery
